title: move deepseek to Azure FW deployment and kimi to Bedrock
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Smoother chats, stronger clouds
gist.blurb: We've rewired the backend for DeepSeek and Kimi to use Azure and AWS Bedrock. Expect faster responses and fewer timeouts when chatting with these models.
gist.summary: Migrated the backend routing for DeepSeek and Kimi models to Azure and AWS Bedrock to resolve timeout issues and improve overall stability.
gist.impact: Chatting with DeepSeek and Kimi is now significantly more reliable, with fewer dropped connections and faster response times.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a network engineer, routing glowing data cables into sturdy, reliable server racks. Nomnom creature happily eating the old, timed-out error logs to show the elimination of timeouts.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny construction hardhat, plugging glowing data cables into a sturdy retro server rack. The round Nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating a stack of red 'timeout' error logs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
